speaker: TALBOT
text: Where is my other life? Mine own is gone.

speaker: TALBOT
text: O, where’s young Talbot? Where is valiant John?

speaker: TALBOT
text: Triumphant Death, smeared with captivity,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Young Talbot’s valor makes me smile at thee.

speaker: TALBOT
text: When he perceived me shrink and on my knee,

speaker: TALBOT
text: His bloody sword he brandished over me,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And like a hungry lion did commence

speaker: TALBOT
text: Rough deeds of rage and stern impatience;

speaker: TALBOT
text: But when my angry guardant stood alone,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Tend’ring my ruin and assailed of none,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Dizzy-eyed fury and great rage of heart

speaker: TALBOT
text: Suddenly made him from my side to start

speaker: TALBOT
text: Into the clust’ring battle of the French;

speaker: TALBOT
text: And in that sea of blood, my boy did drench

speaker: TALBOT
text: His over-mounting spirit; and there died

speaker: TALBOT
text: My Icarus, my blossom, in his pride.

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: O, my dear lord, lo where your son is borne!

speaker: TALBOT
text: Thou antic Death, which laugh’st us here to scorn,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Anon from thy insulting tyranny,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Coupled in bonds of perpetuity,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Two Talbots, wingèd through the lither sky,

speaker: TALBOT
text: In thy despite shall scape mortality.—

speaker: TALBOT
text: O, thou whose wounds become hard-favored Death,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Speak to thy father ere thou yield thy breath!

speaker: TALBOT
text: Brave Death by speaking, whither he will or no.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Imagine him a Frenchman and thy foe.—

speaker: TALBOT
text: Poor boy, he smiles, methinks, as who should say

speaker: TALBOT
text: “Had Death been French, then Death had died today.”—

speaker: TALBOT
text: Come, come, and lay him in his father’s arms;

speaker: TALBOT
text: My spirit can no longer bear these harms.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Soldiers, adieu! I have what I would have,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Now my old arms are young John Talbot’s grave.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Had York and Somerset brought rescue in,

speaker: CHARLES
text: We should have found a bloody day of this.

speaker: BASTARD
text: How the young whelp of Talbot’s, raging wood,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Did flesh his puny sword in Frenchmen’s blood!

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Once I encountered him, and thus I said:

speaker: PUCELLE
text: “Thou maiden youth, be vanquished by a maid.”

speaker: PUCELLE
text: But with a proud majestical high scorn

speaker: PUCELLE
text: He answered thus: “Young Talbot was not born

speaker: PUCELLE
text: To be the pillage of a giglot wench.”

speaker: PUCELLE
text: So, rushing in the bowels of the French,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: He left me proudly, as unworthy fight.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Doubtless he would have made a noble knight.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: See where he lies inhearsèd in the arms

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Of the most bloody nurser of his harms.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Hew them to pieces, hack their bones asunder,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Whose life was England’s glory, Gallia’s wonder.

speaker: CHARLES
text: O, no, forbear! For that which we have fled

speaker: CHARLES
text: During the life, let us not wrong it dead.

speaker: LUCY
text: Herald, conduct me to the Dauphin’s tent,

speaker: LUCY
text: To know who hath obtained the glory of the day.

speaker: CHARLES
text: On what submissive message art thou sent?

speaker: LUCY
text: Submission, dauphin? ’Tis a mere French word.

speaker: LUCY
text: We English warriors wot not what it means.

speaker: LUCY
text: I come to know what prisoners thou hast ta’en,

speaker: LUCY
text: And to survey the bodies of the dead.

speaker: CHARLES
text: For prisoners askst thou? Hell our prison is.

speaker: CHARLES
text: But tell me whom thou seek’st.

speaker: LUCY
text: But where’s the great Alcides of the field,

speaker: LUCY
text: Valiant Lord Talbot, Earl of Shrewsbury,

speaker: LUCY
text: Created for his rare success in arms

speaker: LUCY
text: Great Earl of Washford, Waterford, and Valence,

speaker: LUCY
text: Lord Talbot of Goodrich and Urchinfield,

speaker: LUCY
text: Lord Strange of Blackmere, Lord Verdon of Alton,

speaker: LUCY
text: Lord Cromwell of Wingfield, Lord Furnival of Sheffield,

speaker: LUCY
text: The thrice victorious Lord of Falconbridge,

speaker: LUCY
text: Knight of the noble Order of Saint George,

speaker: LUCY
text: Worthy Saint Michael, and the Golden Fleece,

speaker: LUCY
text: Great Marshal to Henry the Sixth

speaker: LUCY
text: Of all his wars within the realm of France?

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Here’s a silly stately style indeed.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: The Turk, that two-and-fifty kingdoms hath,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Writes not so tedious a style as this.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Him that thou magnifi’st with all these titles

speaker: PUCELLE
text: Stinking and flyblown lies here at our feet.

speaker: LUCY
text: Is Talbot slain, the Frenchmen’s only scourge,

speaker: LUCY
text: Your kingdom’s terror and black Nemesis?

speaker: LUCY
text: O, were mine eyeballs into bullets turned

speaker: LUCY
text: That I in rage might shoot them at your faces!

speaker: LUCY
text: O, that I could but call these dead to life,

speaker: LUCY
text: It were enough to fright the realm of France.

speaker: LUCY
text: Were but his picture left amongst you here,

speaker: LUCY
text: It would amaze the proudest of you all.

speaker: LUCY
text: Give me their bodies, that I may bear them hence

speaker: LUCY
text: And give them burial as beseems their worth.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: I think this upstart is old Talbot’s ghost,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: He speaks with such a proud commanding spirit.

speaker: PUCELLE
text: For God’s sake, let him have him. To keep them here,

speaker: PUCELLE
text: They would but stink and putrefy the air.

speaker: CHARLES
text: Go, take their bodies hence.

speaker: LUCY
text: I’ll bear them hence.

speaker: LUCY
text: But from their ashes shall be reared

speaker: LUCY
text: A phoenix that shall make all France afeard.

speaker: CHARLES
text: So we be rid of them, do with him what thou wilt.

speaker: CHARLES
text: And now to Paris in this conquering vein.

speaker: CHARLES
text: All will be ours, now bloody Talbot’s slain.
